The Library will be closed on Monday, May 25 for Memorial Day.
Why Abbot Matters Learn more about Abbot Library’s services, impact, and current funding considerations.
Borrow/ USEWith a library card, you can access a large collection of print and digital resources.
• Borrow free e-books, audiobooks, Chromebooks, and more!• Get passes and promo codes to select local museums• Reserve a computer or meeting room
Sign up for a card today!
VISITThe newly renovated space includes a redesigned main level with three small meeting rooms.
The lower level features the Killam Children’s Room and Killam Teen Center, the Carten Gallery, the Thompson Makerspace and an Event Center with access to the courtyard and a catering kitchen on the lower level.
ATTENDAbbot Public Library hosts many events of interest to community members of all ages! We are a growing hub for the Marblehead community.
Click the link below for ourschedule of events.

To limit museum types being displayed, check the corresponding categories:
To select a museum, click “Details/Reserve”, then select which Pass to reserve.

Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children."} Boston Children's Museum 308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210617-426-6500http://www.bostonchildrensmuseum.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston Children's Museum Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
Categories: Children, Crafts, Family, Technology
Pass Type: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
Pass Benefits Pass admits up to 4 people at half price.
Regular admission rate is $20 per person. Children 0-12 months are free.
Reserve a Pass Reserve a Pass
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "25 Harbor Shore Drive, Boston, MA 02210","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-institute-of-contemporary-art-boston_13_md.jpg","telephone": "617-478-3100","name": "Institute of Contemporary Art/Boston","url": "","description": "The building that houses the Institute of Contemporary Art/Boston on Fan Pier is the first in the US designed by the architectural firm of Diller Scofidio + Renfro. The museum was founded in 1936 as a sister institution to New York&apos;s MoMA, though it had no permanent collection until starting one after moving into the new building on the waterfront in 2006. In addition to the collection, changing exhibitions, music, dance, film, talks, tours, family activities, and teen programming are offered throughout the year. "} Institute of Contemporary Art/Boston 25 Harbor Shore Drive, Boston, MA 02210617-478-3100https://www.icaboston.org/[Details/Reserve]
Institute of Contemporary Art/Boston The building that houses the Institute of Contemporary Art/Boston on Fan Pier is the first in the US designed by the architectural firm of Diller Scofidio + Renfro. The museum was founded in 1936 as a sister institution to New York's MoMA, though it had no permanent collection until starting one after moving into the new building on the waterfront in 2006. In addition to the collection, changing exhibitions, music, dance, film, talks, tours, family activities, and teen programming are offered throughout the year.
Categories: Art, Family, Music, Dance
Pass Benefits THE CONFRIMATION EMAIL FOR RESERVING THIS PASS IS YOUR LIBRARY PASS.
Pass admits up to 2 visitors at the discounted price of $10 when redeemed at the Holly and David Bruce Visitor Center.
The ICA no longer requires timed entry for admission to the museum, however we strongly recommend reserving tickets ahead of your visit. Please note the following:
1.  Patrons must first acquire a valid digital library pass at their participating library to receive this discount.
2.  Purchase tickets online: https://www.icaboston.org/
3.  Click on “Buy Tickets” and select your chosen date for ICA General Museum Admission
4.  Click on “Library Pass Coupon” ticket price. Anyone 18 and under receive complimentary admission and should use the ticket price “Youth (0-12)” or “Youth (13-18)”
5.  Follow steps to complete the order. Your ICA tickets will be emailed to you.
6.  When you arrive at the ICA, proceed to the visitor services desk with your tickets and your library pass. The library pass will be collected at this time (if physical) or shown to the staff member at the desk (if digital). If you have questions about the online ordering system, contact visitorservices@icaboston.org.
Your ticket is valid for entry any time during the date selected. Note: Final museum entry is 20 minutes before closing. Tickets are final sale.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "25 Evans Way, Boston, MA 02115","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-gardner-museum_14_md.jpg","telephone": "617-566-1401","name": "Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum","url": "","description": "The Gardner Museum houses the collection of Isabella Stewart Gardner, a local patron of the arts who died in 1924, in a building she commissioned to look like a 15th century Venetian palazzo. The center of the original building showcases courtyard plantings that reflect the seasons as they evolve throughout the year. In addition to her collection, which remains displayed exactly as she arranged it per her will, there are changing exhibitions, concerts, lectures, family events, and an artist-in-residence program. In 2012, the museum opened an addition designed by Renzo Piano that houses a space for their concert series. "} Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum 25 Evans Way, Boston, MA 02115617-566-1401http://www.gardnermuseum.org/home/[Details/Reserve]
Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum The Gardner Museum houses the collection of Isabella Stewart Gardner, a local patron of the arts who died in 1924, in a building she commissioned to look like a 15th century Venetian palazzo. The center of the original building showcases courtyard plantings that reflect the seasons as they evolve throughout the year. In addition to her collection, which remains displayed exactly as she arranged it per her will, there are changing exhibitions, concerts, lectures, family events, and an artist-in-residence program. In 2012, the museum opened an addition designed by Renzo Piano that houses a space for their concert series.
Categories: Art, Family, Music
Pass Benefits THIS IS AN ONLINE PASS. IT WILL BE E-MAILED TO YOU UPON RESERVING IT. 
This library pass does not guarantee a reservation to the Museum. If you have any issues reserving your tickets, you can call the Gardner Museum's Box Office at 617 278 5156.
The library pass allows you to get up to four tickets at $5 each (choose the Library Pass ticket type). Any additional ticket will be normally priced (Children under 18 are always free and should be reserved as child tickets)
PATRONS MUST PRESENT THIS LIBRARY CODE FOR ENTRY TO THE MUSEUM
For more information, including health and safety requirements, please visit isgm.org/welcome
